title: Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors
subtitle: Net [official development assistance](#dod:oda) to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.
caption: Across the globe in 2023, net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors varies widely, with the highest flows concentrated in parts of Africa and South Asia and the lowest or absent across most of the Americas, Europe, and Australia. The map uses a continuous color scale ranging from pale yellow for $0 to deep navy for $300 million or more, with intermediate bands at $10 million, $30 million, and $100 million. Countries receiving the largest amounts — falling in the darkest blue band — include Ethiopia, Nigeria, Kenya, and India. Several nations in East and Southern Africa — Tanzania, Uganda, Mozambique — along with Pakistan and Bangladesh, sit in the next tier, colored medium to dark teal, indicating receipts between $100 million and $300 million. In the middle range, countries like Colombia, Peru, Indonesia, and Morocco appear in lighter teal shades, corresponding to the $30 million to $100 million band. Lower recipient levels — pale green to light yellow — cover much of Latin America (Argentina, Chile, Mexico), North Africa (Egypt, Tunisia), and parts of Southeast Asia (Thailand, Vietnam). Vast regions show no data: all of North America, Western Europe, Russia, China, Australia, and New Zealand are shaded with diagonal hatching, meaning they are not recipients of this category of aid. The geographic pattern shows that assistance is heavily directed toward sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia, while middle-income regions receive modest amounts and high-income economies receive none. The distribution forms a clear gradient from deep blue cores in East and West Africa and the Indian subcontinent outward to pale greens in Latin America and North Africa, with large swaths of the Global North entirely excluded from the flow.
